chunks.text: IN THE HIGH COURT OF SINDH BENCH AT SUKKUR Constitution Petition No. D 1158 of 2024 DATE OF HEARING ORDER WITH SIGNATURE OF JUDGE 1. For orders on CMA No. 4515/2024 (U/A) 2. For orders on office objection at flag ‘A’ 3. For hearing of CMA No. 4516/2024 (Ex/A) 4. For orders on CMA No. 4517/2024 (stay) 5. For hearing of main case 22.08.2024 Mr. Wajid Ali Shaikh, Advocate for petitioner ************** 1. Urgent applications stands granted. 2. Not considered as the matter is being disposed of. 3. Exemption is granted, subject to all just legal exceptions. 4&5. Learned counsel for the petitioner contends that the respondent No.6 has acquired an unavailable FIR against petitioner from the concerned respondent despite of fact that daughter of petitioner having a family dispute with the respondent No.6, proceedings filed by way of Family Suit bearing No. 210/2023 pending before the Consumer Protection Court Sukkur, he further submits that petitioner has acquired bail in the said matter, however challan not being submitted in said FIR. She has submitted application to higher officials being respondents No.2 and 3 for re-investigation of the matter and presence only requires that said grievance be addressed. Mr. Shafi Muhammad Mahar, learned Deputy Prosecutor General present in the Court was pleased to waive the notice and conceded to required order be passed by the concerned official within a period of one week. Ordered accordingly. In case subject application has not been decided, the concerned officer shall pass an order in accordance with merits of the matter without fail within said period of one week. Petition stands disposed in the above terms.

In case subject application has not been decided, the concerned officer shall pass an order in accordance with merits of the matter without fail within said period of one week. Petition stands disposed in the above terms. J U D G E J U D G E M. Ali/steno*
